title: random stuff from evastore ebay and other outlets
display_title: random stuff from evastore ebay and other outlets
wikitext:
[]: SALE, SALE, SALE!             Quote Post by ice reaper » Wed Jul 27, 2005 8:41 am http://www.toysnjoys.com/eva_artbooks.html there goes my holiday money....

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Jul 27, 2005 8:44 am Groundwork? For Sale? Is this a dagger I see before me?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 27, 2005 10:51 am Just so you people know, the "Neon Genesis Evangelion Illustration and Artwork Collection" is a bootleg book. Don't buy it. It is eViL.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Wed Jul 27, 2005 11:01 am What's the big thing in the background here ?

[]:             Quote Post by Machinehead » Wed Jul 27, 2005 3:49 pm Ak! I want this one.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 27, 2005 5:28 pm Machinehead wrote: Ak! I want this one.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 27, 2005 6:01 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: That one can be purchased for far less on sites such as JPQueen . In fact many of these books can be found for less elsewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Ataru » Sun Jul 31, 2005 1:59 pm toysnjoys seem to sell a ton of bootleg shit, look at the Totoro plushes, and look at the prices. The offical Studio Ghibli totoro plushes I own were expensive as hell ($15 for a small Totoro) and are astoundingly high quality. The bootleg Totoro plushes I own were super cheap and are of dubious quality; they look exactly like the photos toysnjoys are showing, except the toysnjoys ones are even worse in construction. Hideous looking. So, in short, I would expect these nicely priced Evangelion books are almost certainly bootlegs.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 31, 2005 4:17 pm As Monkey said, you can buy the genuine articles for less other places. In my experience, the only Eva books that are bootleg are the ones that were bootleg to begin with, such as that one title I pointed out earlier. (Now, music CDs, on the other hand...)

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Mon Aug 08, 2005 7:20 am ...........here we go, get ready. what's bootleg? there i said it. and why do you think/know why these are such legs of the mighty boot

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Aug 08, 2005 1:36 pm ice reaper wrote: what's bootleg?

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Wed Aug 10, 2005 6:48 am thanks

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Wed Aug 10, 2005 2:05 pm Why Der mond is nowhere? :cry: And at this site, the Groundworks-books seem to be cheap..Are they original?

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Wed Aug 10, 2005 9:42 pm the_seventh_child wrote: Why Der mond is nowhere? :cry: And at this site, the Groundworks-books seem to be cheap..Are they original?

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Aug 12, 2005 3:36 am Digitalex wrote: I ordered my Der Monde (and Die Sterne) from Amazon Germany. I think I have a better chance of translating German to English then Japanese to English.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Fri Aug 12, 2005 7:21 am i even seen der mond for 60 $ !!!

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Fri Aug 12, 2005 9:52 am the_seventh_child wrote: Digitalex wrote: I ordered my Der Monde (and Die Sterne) from Amazon Germany. I think I have a better chance of translating German to English then Japanese to English.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Aug 12, 2005 10:21 am ice reaper wrote: i've seen it (and tried to buy it) for the equivelant of.......about.......$90. i want it soooo bad, but carl hasn't been on in so long and he was trying to get it or me :cry:

[]:             Quote Post by Digitalex » Fri Aug 12, 2005 10:26 am the_seventh_child wrote: ice reaper wrote: i've seen it (and tried to buy it) for the equivelant of.......about.......$90. i want it soooo bad, but carl hasn't been on in so long and he was trying to get it or me :cry:

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Aug 12, 2005 10:32 am Digitalex wrote: Once I get it translated I'll post about it. I just need to get hold of my German friend first.
